1	# /release — Automated Release Workflow
2	
3	**Arguments**: $ARGUMENTS
4	
5	You are executing the release workflow for `@code-insights/cli`. Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to extract:
6	- **type** (required): `patch`, `minor`, or `major`
7	- **description** (optional): A one-liner for the release title
8	
9	If type is missing or not one of `patch`/`minor`/`major`, ask the user to provide it.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Step 1: Pre-flight Checks
14	
15	Run ALL of these checks. If any fail, STOP and tell the user what to fix.
16	
17	```bash
18	# Must be on master
19	git branch --show-current  # Must output "master"
20	
21	# Must have clean working tree
22	git status --porcelain     # Must be empty
23	
24	# Pull latest
25	git pull origin master
26	```
27	
28	Read the current version from `cli/package.json` (the `"version"` field).
29	
30	Compute the new version:
31	- `patch`: increment the third number (3.6.1 → 3.6.2)
32	- `minor`: increment the second number, reset third to 0 (3.6.1 → 3.7.0)
33	- `major`: increment the first number, reset second and third to 0 (3.6.1 → 4.0.0)
34	
35	**Resume detection**: Run `npm view @code-insights/cli version` to get the currently published version. If the version in `package.json` is ALREADY GREATER than the npm version, this is a **resumed release** — skip to Step 6.
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Step 2: Analyze Changes
40	
41	Find the last git tag and analyze what changed:
42	
43	```bash
44	# Find latest tag
45	git describe --tags --abbrev=0
46	
47	# Commits since last tag
48	git log $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --oneline
49	
50	# Full diff since last tag
51	git diff $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --stat
52	```
53	
54	Read the full diff and commit messages. Then:
55	
56	1. **Generate a CHANGELOG entry** in the existing format (see `cli/CHANGELOG.md` for style). Group changes under `### Added`, `### Changed`, `### Fixed`, `### Improved` as appropriate. Match the existing tone — concise but descriptive, with bold lead-ins.
57	
58	2. **Generate a release title** — a short phrase summarizing the release (e.g., "LLM-Powered Export Page", "PostHog custom domain"). Use the user's description if provided, otherwise derive from the changes.
59	
60	3. **For minor/major releases**: Assess which docs need updating:
61	   - `docs/ROADMAP.md` — if new milestones were completed
62	   - `docs/PRODUCT.md` — if product capabilities changed
63	   - `docs/VISION.md` — for major releases, if the vision expanded
64	   - `README.md` — if user-facing features or setup changed
65	
66	---
67	
68	## GATE 1: Review Changelog
69	
70	**STOP and present to the user:**
71	
72	```
73	Release: vX.Y.Z — {title}
74	Type: {patch|minor|major}
75	
76	Proposed CHANGELOG entry:
77	─────────────────────────
78	{the generated changelog entry}
79	─────────────────────────
80	
81	Files to modify:
82	  - cli/package.json (version bump)
83	  - cli/CHANGELOG.md (new entry)
84	  {- docs/ROADMAP.md (if applicable)}
85	  {- docs/PRODUCT.md (if applicable)}
86	  {- docs/VISION.md (if applicable)}
87	
88	Approve, or tell me what to change?
89	```
90	
91	Wait for user approval. If they provide edits, incorporate them.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## Step 3: Apply Version Bump
96	
97	After approval, make the changes:
98	
99	1. **`cli/package.json`** — Update the `"version"` field to the new version
100	2. **`cli/CHANGELOG.md`** — Prepend the new entry at the top (after the header, before the previous version entry). Format: `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD`
101	3. **Docs** (if approved at Gate 1) — Make the specific updates discussed
102	
103	---
104	
105	## Step 4: Build & Test
106	
107	Run the full build and test suite:
108	
109	```bash
110	# Full workspace build (this is what prepublishOnly does)
111	cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights && pnpm build
112	
113	# Run tests
114	cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli && pnpm test
115	```
116	
117	**If build fails → STOP.** Show the error. Do not continue.
118	**If tests fail → STOP.** Show the error. Do not continue.
119	
120	---
121	
122	## GATE 2: Confirm Publish
123	
124	**STOP and present to the user:**
125	
126	Show the full `git diff` of all changes made, then:
127	
128	```
129	Build: PASSED
130	Tests: PASSED
131	
132	Ready to:
133	  1. Commit changes to master
134	  2. Push to origin
135	  3. Publish v{X.Y.Z} to npm
136	  4. Create GitHub release
137	
138	Proceed?
139	```
140	
141	Wait for explicit user approval before continuing.
142	
143	---
144	
145	## Step 5: Commit & Push
146	
147	Commit with specific files only (never `git add .`):
148	
149	```bash
150	# Add only the files we changed
151	git add cli/package.json cli/CHANGELOG.md
152	# Add any docs files that were modified (if applicable)
153	# git add docs/ROADMAP.md docs/PRODUCT.md docs/VISION.md README.md
154	
155	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
156	chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z
157	
158	{one-line summary of what changed}
159	
160	Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
161	EOF
162	)"
163	
164	git push origin master
165	```
166	
167	---
168	
169	## Step 6: Publish to npm
170	
171	```bash
172	cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli && npm publish
173	```
174	
175	The `prepublishOnly` script in `package.json` handles building all packages and copying dashboard/server dist files automatically.
176	
177	Verify the publish succeeded:
178	
179	```bash
180	npm view @code-insights/cli version
181	```
182	
183	If publish fails, show the error and STOP. The commit is already pushed — tell the user to fix the issue and re-run `/release` (resume detection will skip to this step).
184	
185	---
186	
187	## Step 7: GitHub Release
188	
189	Create the GitHub release using the changelog entry as the body:
190	
191	```bash
192	gh release create vX.Y.Z \
193	  --title "vX.Y.Z — {release title}" \
194	  --notes "$(cat <<'EOF'
195	{changelog entry content, formatted for GitHub}
196	
197	**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/compare/{previous_tag}...vX.Y.Z
198	EOF
199	)"
200	```
201	
202	---
203	
204	## GATE 3: Verify
205	
206	**STOP and present to the user:**
207	
208	```
209	Release vX.Y.Z complete!
210	
211	  npm: @code-insights/cli@X.Y.Z ✓
212	  GitHub: {release URL}
213	  Commit: {commit hash}
214	
215	Everything look correct?
216	```
217	
218	---
219	
220	## Step 8: Done
221	
222	Summarize the release:
223	
224	```
225	Released @code-insights/cli vX.Y.Z
226	
227	  Changes: {brief summary}
228	  npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@code-insights/cli
229	  GitHub: {release URL}
230	  Files modified: {list}
231	```
232	
233	---
234	
235	## Important Rules
236	
237	- **NEVER skip the gates** — always pause for user confirmation
238	- **NEVER use `git add .` or `git add -A`** — add specific files only
239	- **If build or tests fail, STOP** — do not try to fix and continue
240	- **Commit message format**: `chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z` (matches existing pattern)
241	- **Resume is safe** — if re-run after a partial release, it picks up where it left off

file.content:
# /release — Automated Release Workflow

**Arguments**: $ARGUMENTS

You are executing the release workflow for `@code-insights/cli`. Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to extract:
- **type** (required): `patch`, `minor`, or `major`
- **description** (optional): A one-liner for the release title

If type is missing or not one of `patch`/`minor`/`major`, ask the user to provide it.

---

## Step 1: Pre-flight Checks

Run ALL of these checks. If any fail, STOP and tell the user what to fix.

```bash
# Must be on master
git branch --show-current  # Must output "master"

# Must have clean working tree
git status --porcelain     # Must be empty

# Pull latest
git pull origin master
```

Read the current version from `cli/package.json` (the `"version"` field).

Compute the new version:
- `patch`: increment the third number (3.6.1 → 3.6.2)
- `minor`: increment the second number, reset third to 0 (3.6.1 → 3.7.0)
- `major`: increment the first number, reset second and third to 0 (3.6.1 → 4.0.0)

**Resume detection**: Run `npm view @code-insights/cli version` to get the currently published version. If the version in `package.json` is ALREADY GREATER than the npm version, this is a **resumed release** — skip to Step 6.

---

## Step 2: Analyze Changes

Find the last git tag and analyze what changed:

```bash
# Find latest tag
git describe --tags --abbrev=0

# Commits since last tag
git log $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --oneline

# Full diff since last tag
git diff $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --stat
```

Read the full diff and commit messages. Then:

1. **Generate a CHANGELOG entry** in the existing format (see `cli/CHANGELOG.md` for style). Group changes under `### Added`, `### Changed`, `### Fixed`, `### Improved` as appropriate. Match the existing tone — concise but descriptive, with bold lead-ins.

2. **Generate a release title** — a short phrase summarizing the release (e.g., "LLM-Powered Export Page", "PostHog custom domain"). Use the user's description if provided, otherwise derive from the changes.

3. **For minor/major releases**: Assess which docs need updating:
   - `docs/ROADMAP.md` — if new milestones were completed
   - `docs/PRODUCT.md` — if product capabilities changed
   - `docs/VISION.md` — for major releases, if the vision expanded
   - `README.md` — if user-facing features or setup changed

---

## GATE 1: Review Changelog

**STOP and present to the user:**

```
Release: vX.Y.Z — {title}
Type: {patch|minor|major}

Proposed CHANGELOG entry:
─────────────────────────
{the generated changelog entry}
─────────────────────────

Files to modify:
  - cli/package.json (version bump)
  - cli/CHANGELOG.md (new entry)
  {- docs/ROADMAP.md (if applicable)}
  {- docs/PRODUCT.md (if applicable)}
  {- docs/VISION.md (if applicable)}

Approve, or tell me what to change?
```

Wait for user approval. If they provide edits, incorporate them.

---

## Step 3: Apply Version Bump

After approval, make the changes:

1. **`cli/package.json`** — Update the `"version"` field to the new version
2. **`cli/CHANGELOG.md`** — Prepend the new entry at the top (after the header, before the previous version entry). Format: `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD`
3. **Docs** (if approved at Gate 1) — Make the specific updates discussed

---

## Step 4: Build & Test

Run the full build and test suite:

```bash
# Full workspace build (this is what prepublishOnly does)
cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights && pnpm build

# Run tests
cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli && pnpm test
```

**If build fails → STOP.** Show the error. Do not continue.
**If tests fail → STOP.** Show the error. Do not continue.

---

## GATE 2: Confirm Publish

**STOP and present to the user:**

Show the full `git diff` of all changes made, then:

```
Build: PASSED
Tests: PASSED

Ready to:
  1. Commit changes to master
  2. Push to origin
  3. Publish v{X.Y.Z} to npm
  4. Create GitHub release

Proceed?
```

Wait for explicit user approval before continuing.

---

## Step 5: Commit & Push

Commit with specific files only (never `git add .`):

```bash
# Add only the files we changed
git add cli/package.json cli/CHANGELOG.md
# Add any docs files that were modified (if applicable)
# git add docs/ROADMAP.md docs/PRODUCT.md docs/VISION.md README.md

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z

{one-line summary of what changed}

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git push origin master
```

---

## Step 6: Publish to npm

```bash
cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli && npm publish
```

The `prepublishOnly` script in `package.json` handles building all packages and copying dashboard/server dist files automatically.

Verify the publish succeeded:

```bash
npm view @code-insights/cli version
```

If publish fails, show the error and STOP. The commit is already pushed — tell the user to fix the issue and re-run `/release` (resume detection will skip to this step).

---

## Step 7: GitHub Release

Create the GitHub release using the changelog entry as the body:

```bash
gh release create vX.Y.Z \
  --title "vX.Y.Z — {release title}" \
  --notes "$(cat <<'EOF'
{changelog entry content, formatted for GitHub}

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/compare/{previous_tag}...vX.Y.Z
EOF
)"
```

---

## GATE 3: Verify

**STOP and present to the user:**

```
Release vX.Y.Z complete!

  npm: @code-insights/cli@X.Y.Z ✓
  GitHub: {release URL}
  Commit: {commit hash}

Everything look correct?
```

---

## Step 8: Done

Summarize the release:

```
Released @code-insights/cli vX.Y.Z

  Changes: {brief summary}
  npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@code-insights/cli
  GitHub: {release URL}
  Files modified: {list}
```

---

## Important Rules

- **NEVER skip the gates** — always pause for user confirmation
- **NEVER use `git add .` or `git add -A`** — add specific files only
- **If build or tests fail, STOP** — do not try to fix and continue
- **Commit message format**: `chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z` (matches existing pattern)
- **Resume is safe** — if re-run after a partial release, it picks up where it left off

Here's what I see in the current release skill:

- **Step 2** (line 63) mentions `README.md` for minor/major releases, but `cli/README.md` is never mentioned
- **Gate 1** lists files to modify but doesn't explicitly include `cli/README.md`
- **Step 5** has a commented-out `git add` for `README.md` but not `cli/README.md`

**Proposed changes:**

1. **Step 2, point 3** — Add `cli/README.md` to the docs assessment list alongside `README.md`, and apply to all release types (not just minor/major)
2. **Gate 1** — Include both READMEs in the "Files to modify" list
3. **Step 5** — Include `cli/README.md` in the commented git add line

Want me to go ahead?

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The release skill (`.claude/commands/release.md`) is a structured workflow with **gates** — explicit pause points requiring user approval. This pattern prevents automation from doing irreversible things (publishing to npm, pushing to master) without human review. Adding `cli/README.md` to the checklist ensures the npm package's own README stays current with each release.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

   - `cli/README.md` — if CLI usage, commands, or npm package description changed

2. **Generate a release title** — a short phrase summarizing the release (e.g., "LLM-Powered Export Page", "PostHog custom domain"). Use the user's description if provided, otherwise derive from the changes.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# /release — Automated Release Workflow

**Arguments**: $ARGUMENTS

You are executing the release workflow for `@code-insights/cli`. Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to extract:
- **type** (required): `patch`, `minor`, or `major`
- **description** (optional): A one-liner for the release title

If type is missing or not one of `patch`/`minor`/`major`, ask the user to provide it.

---

## Step 1: Pre-flight Checks

Run ALL of these checks. If any fail, STOP and tell the user what to fix.

```bash
# Must be on master
git branch --show-current  # Must output "master"

# Must have clean working tree
git status --porcelain     # Must be empty

# Pull latest
git pull origin master
```

Read the current version from `cli/package.json` (the `"version"` field).

Compute the new version:
- `patch`: increment the third number (3.6.1 → 3.6.2)
- `minor`: increment the second number, reset third to 0 (3.6.1 → 3.7.0)
- `major`: increment the first number, reset second and third to 0 (3.6.1 → 4.0.0)

**Resume detection**: Run `npm view @code-insights/cli version` to get the currently published version. If the version in `package.json` is ALREADY GREATER than the npm version, this is a **resumed release** — skip to Step 6.

---

## Step 2: Analyze Changes

Find the last git tag and analyze what changed:

```bash
# Find latest tag
git describe --tags --abbrev=0

# Commits since last tag
git log $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --oneline

# Full diff since last tag
git diff $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --stat
```

Read the full diff and commit messages. Then:

1. **Generate a CHANGELOG entry** in the existing format (see `cli/CHANGELOG.md` for style). Group changes under `### Added`, `### Changed`, `### Fixed`, `### Improved` as appropriate. Match the existing tone — concise but descriptive, with bold lead-ins.

2. **Generate a release title** — a short phrase summarizing the release (e.g., "LLM-Powered Export Page", "PostHog custom domain"). Use the user's description if provided, otherwise derive from the changes.

3. **For minor/major releases**: Assess which docs need updating:
   - `docs/ROADMAP.md` — if new milestones were completed
   - `docs/PRODUCT.md` — if product capabilities changed
   - `docs/VISION.md` — for major releases, if the vision expanded
   - `README.md` — if user-facing features or setup changed
   - `cli/README.md` — if CLI usage, commands, or npm package description changed

---

## GATE 1: Review Changelog

**STOP and present to the user:**

```
Release: vX.Y.Z — {title}
Type: {patch|minor|major}

Proposed CHANGELOG entry:
─────────────────────────
{the generated changelog entry}
─────────────────────────

Files to modify:
  - cli/package.json (version bump)
  - cli/CHANGELOG.md (new entry)
  {- docs/ROADMAP.md (if applicable)}
  {- docs/PRODUCT.md (if applicable)}
  {- docs/VISION.md (if applicable)}

Approve, or tell me what to change?
```

Wait for user approval. If they provide edits, incorporate them.

---

## Step 3: Apply Version Bump

After approval, make the changes:

1. **`cli/package.json`** — Update the `"version"` field to the new version
2. **`cli/CHANGELOG.md`** — Prepend the new entry at the top (after the header, before the previous version entry). Format: `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD`
3. **Docs** (if approved at Gate 1) — Make the specific updates discussed

---

## Step 4: Build & Test

Run the full build and test suite:

```bash
# Full workspace build (this is what prepublishOnly does)
cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights && pnpm build

# Run tests
cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli && pnpm test
```

**If build fails → STOP.** Show the error. Do not continue.
**If tests fail → STOP.** Show the error. Do not continue.

---

## GATE 2: Confirm Publish

**STOP and present to the user:**

Show the full `git diff` of all changes made, then:

```
Build: PASSED
Tests: PASSED

Ready to:
  1. Commit changes to master
  2. Push to origin
  3. Publish v{X.Y.Z} to npm
  4. Create GitHub release

Proceed?
```

Wait for explicit user approval before continuing.

---

## Step 5: Commit & Push

Commit with specific files only (never `git add .`):

```bash
# Add only the files we changed
git add cli/package.json cli/CHANGELOG.md
# Add any docs files that were modified (if applicable)
# git add docs/ROADMAP.md docs/PRODUCT.md docs/VISION.md README.md

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z

{one-line summary of what changed}

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git push origin master
```

---

## Step 6: Publish to npm

```bash
cd /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli && npm publish
```

The `prepublishOnly` script in `package.json` handles building all packages and copying dashboard/server dist files automatically.

Verify the publish succeeded:

```bash
npm view @code-insights/cli version
```

If publish fails, show the error and STOP. The commit is already pushed — tell the user to fix the issue and re-run `/release` (resume detection will skip to this step).

---

## Step 7: GitHub Release

Create the GitHub release using the changelog entry as the body:

```bash
gh release create vX.Y.Z \
  --title "vX.Y.Z — {release title}" \
  --notes "$(cat <<'EOF'
{changelog entry content, formatted for GitHub}

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/compare/{previous_tag}...vX.Y.Z
EOF
)"
```

---

## GATE 3: Verify

**STOP and present to the user:**

```
Release vX.Y.Z complete!

  npm: @code-insights/cli@X.Y.Z ✓
  GitHub: {release URL}
  Commit: {commit hash}

Everything look correct?
```

---

## Step 8: Done

Summarize the release:

```
Released @code-insights/cli vX.Y.Z

  Changes: {brief summary}
  npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@code-insights/cli
  GitHub: {release URL}
  Files modified: {list}
```

---

## Important Rules

- **NEVER skip the gates** — always pause for user confirmation
- **NEVER use `git add .` or `git add -A`** — add specific files only
- **If build or tests fail, STOP** — do not try to fix and continue
- **Commit message format**: `chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z` (matches existing pattern)
- **Resume is safe** — if re-run after a partial release, it picks up where it left off

content.text:
All three edits are done. Here's a summary of what changed in `.claude/commands/release.md`:

1. **Step 2** — Added `cli/README.md` to the docs assessment checklist with guidance: "if CLI usage, commands, or npm package description changed"
2. **Gate 1** — Added `{- cli/README.md (if applicable)}` to the "Files to modify" list
3. **Step 5** — Added `cli/README.md` to the commented `git add` line

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- `README.md` (repo root) is what GitHub displays on the repo landing page — it's for contributors and visitors browsing the source
- `cli/README.md` is what **npm displays** on the package page (`npmjs.com/package/@code-insights/cli`) — it's what users see when deciding whether to install. Keeping it current with new commands/features is critical for adoption.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The branch `feature/release-readme-check` is ready. Want me to commit and create a PR?

chore: add cli/README.md to release checklist
